title: Cache Construction Types: Direct Mapped, Fully Associative, Set Associative
summary: Describes and compares direct mapped, fully associative, and set associative caches: address decomposition, data placement, and trade-offs.
content_points: Direct mapped: each memory address maps to exactly one cache line; address = tag | line_selector | byte_offset.
Advantages: simple, fast. Drawbacks: conflicts (two addresses mapping to same line evict each other).
Fully associative: any line can go anywhere; implemented with associative (content-addressable) memory.
Advantages: flexible, high hit ratio. Drawbacks: slow, complex hardware.
Set associative: middle ground; address maps to a set of n lines (n-way); best of both worlds.
Set associative: slightly slower than direct mapped; much better hit ratio.

examples.name: Direct mapped address split
examples.description: 32-bit address, 2048 lines × 32 bytes: line_selector=11 bits, byte_offset=5 bits, tag=16 bits.

learning_objectives: Describe direct mapped, fully associative, and set associative cache designs
Split a memory address for a direct mapped cache
Compare the three types in terms of speed and hit ratio
